title: Magnificently Silky Ethiopian Custard Fir Fir
description: A rich and indulgent Ethiopian dish featuring a silky custard and long-fermentation dough, perfect for a special occasion. This ovo-vegetarian friendly recipe is sure to impress.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and yeast, then gradually add the warm water, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic, then place it in a greased bowl, cover it, and let it ferment in a warm place for 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, combine the melted butter, eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla extract, and salt, and blend until smooth.
Pour the custard mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish and set it aside.
Punch down the fermented dough and tear it into small pieces, then add them to the custard mixture, stirring gently to combine.
Bake the fir fir for 35 minutes, or until it is puffed and golden brown.
Remove the fir fir from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the fir fir warm, garnished with a sprinkle of sugar if desired.
